CD4+ T cell.notes: Core T cell markers (CD3 complex) combined with CD4 define this population. CCR7/TCF7 further distinguish naive from memory.

CD8+ T cell.notes: Cytotoxic T cells. GZMB/PRF1 mark effector function. Exhausted CD8+ T cells additionally express PDCD1, HAVCR2, TIGIT.

Regulatory T cell.notes: FOXP3 is the master regulator. IL2RA (CD25) co-expression with FOXP3 is the gold standard. Highly enriched in tumors.

NK cell.notes: CD56 (NCAM1) is canonical. CD56dim (FCGR3A+) are cytotoxic; CD56bright (FCGR3A-) are cytokine producers. No TCR expression.

B cell.notes: CD20 (MS4A1) and CD19 are the clinical standard. CD79A/B are part of the B cell receptor complex.

Plasma cell.notes: Terminally differentiated B cells. Note: CD19/CD20 are downregulated. PRDM1 (Blimp1) is the master regulator.

Monocyte.notes: Classical monocytes (CD14++CD16-). S100A8/S100A9 are highly specific. Non-classical monocytes are CD14+CD16+ (FCGR3A+).

Macrophage.notes: Tissue macrophages vary greatly by location. TREM2+ macrophages are a tumor-associated subset. C1Q complex is broadly reliable.

Dendritic cell.notes: cDC1: CLEC9A+XCR1+. cDC2: CD1C+FCER1A+. pDC: LILRA4+CLEC4C+. IRF8 marks cDC1 lineage commitment.

Mast cell.notes: Tryptase (TPSAB1/TPSB2) is the gold standard. CPA3 is highly specific. KIT (CD117) alone is insufficient — also marks other cells.

Neutrophil.notes: FCGR3B (CD16b) is neutrophil-specific. MPO/ELANE are granule proteins. Hard to capture in standard scRNA-seq due to low RNA content.

Epithelial cell.notes: EpCAM and cytokeratins (KRT8/18/19) are canonical. CDH1 (E-cadherin) marks epithelial identity. Loss of CDH1 indicates EMT.

Club cell.notes: Formerly Clara cells. SCGB1A1 (CC16) is the gold standard lung-specific marker. Found in bronchioles.

Alveolar type I cell.notes: AT1 cells are large, flat, cover 95% of alveolar surface. AGER (RAGE) and PDPN (podoplanin) are canonical.

Alveolar type II cell.notes: Surfactant protein C (SFTPC) is the gold standard AT2 marker. AT2 cells are the lung stem cell population.

Hepatocyte.notes: Albumin (ALB) is canonical. Zonation exists: pericentral (CYP2E1+) vs periportal (PCK1+). HNF4A is the master TF.

Cholangiocyte.notes: Biliary epithelial cells. KRT19+KRT7+ALB- distinguishes from hepatocytes. SOX9 marks biliary progenitors.

Enterocyte.notes: Absorptive cells lining the small intestine. FABP1/2 and brush border enzymes (SI, LCT) are canonical.

Goblet cell.notes: MUC2 is the gold standard mucin for intestinal goblet cells. TFF3 (trefoil factor 3) is highly specific.

Fibroblast.notes: Collagen genes (COL1A1/1A2/3A1) are canonical but not exclusive. FAP marks cancer-associated fibroblasts. ACTA2 marks myofibroblasts.

Endothelial cell.notes: CD31 (PECAM1) and VE-cadherin (CDH5) are the gold standard. PLVAP marks fenestrated endothelium. FLT1 marks tip cells.

Pericyte.notes: RGS5 is highly specific. PDGFRB alone insufficient — also marks fibroblasts. ACTA2 marks contractile pericytes/smooth muscle.

Smooth muscle cell.notes: MYH11 is the most specific smooth muscle marker. ACTA2 (αSMA) is shared with myofibroblasts and pericytes.

Adipocyte.notes: Adiponectin (ADIPOQ) and leptin (LEP) are gold standard. PLIN1 marks lipid droplet-associated protein. PPARG is the master TF.

Neuron.notes: NeuN (RBFOX3) is the gold standard post-mitotic neuron marker. SYP/SYT1 mark synaptic vesicles. DCX marks immature neurons.

Astrocyte.notes: GFAP is canonical but varies by region and reactivity. ALDH1L1 is more broadly expressed. AQP4 marks endfeet. Reactive astrocytes upregulate VIM.

Oligodendrocyte.notes: Myelin genes (MBP, MOG, PLP1) are highly specific. OLIG2 marks both OPC and oligodendrocytes. CNP marks mature myelinating cells.

Oligodendrocyte precursor cell.notes: NG2 (CSPG4) is canonical OPC marker. PDGFRA drives OPC proliferation. Distinguished from mature oligodendrocytes by absence of MBP/MOG.

Microglia.notes: P2RY12 and TMEM119 are highly CNS-specific homeostatic markers, lost in disease-associated microglia (DAM). TREM2 marks DAM subset.

Intestinal stem cell.notes: LGR5 is the definitive intestinal stem cell marker. OLFM4 is highly expressed and more stable than LGR5 in sequencing data.

Hematopoietic stem cell.notes: CD34 is broadly expressed on progenitors. True HSCs are CD34+CD38-CD90+CD45RA-. MLLT3 and SPINK2 are more specific in scRNA-seq.

Cancer-associated fibroblast.notes: FAP is the most specific CAF marker. Multiple CAF subtypes exist: myoCAF (ACTA2+), iCAF (IL6+), apCAF (MHC-II+). Context-dependent.

Tumor-associated macrophage.notes: SPP1+ TAMs are associated with poor prognosis. TREM2+ TAMs are immunosuppressive. M1/M2 polarization is a simplification — TAM states exist on a continuum.

Exhausted T cell.notes: Co-expression of multiple inhibitory receptors defines exhaustion. TOX is a key TF. TCF7+ progenitor exhausted cells can respond to checkpoint blockade.

Erythrocyte.notes: Hemoglobin genes (HBB, HBA1/2) are definitive. Often considered contamination in non-blood tissues. GYPA (glycophorin A) is highly specific.

Megakaryocyte.notes: PF4 (CXCL4) and PPBP (CXCL7) are platelet-derived. GP1BA (CD42b) is the von Willebrand factor receptor.
